# Official Platform Sources

These are the highest-authority sources in the pack because they describe platform-provided ranking signals, metrics, and creator tools.

## TikTok

### How TikTok recommends content

URL: https://support.tiktok.com/en/using-tiktok/exploring-videos/how-tiktok-recommends-content

Key points used:

- TikTok recommendations use user interactions such as likes, shares, comments, full watches, skips, and profile visits.
- Content information includes sounds, hashtags, number of video views, and publishing context.
- User information includes device settings, language preference, location, time zone, and device type.
- TikTok search also weighs how well content matches the search inquiry, plus hashtags, sounds, and relevance.

Research implication:

```text
Optimise for observable viewer behaviour: watch, finish, skip less, share, comment, and match topic/search intent.
```

### TikTok Newsroom: How TikTok recommends videos #ForYou

URL: https://newsroom.tiktok.com/en-us/how-tiktok-recommends-videos-for-you

Key points used:

- Recommendations are based on factors including interactions, video information, and device/account settings.
- Finishing a longer video can be a stronger interest indicator than weaker contextual signals.
- Follower count and previous high-performing videos are not direct recommendation factors.

Research implication:

```text
Do not assume only large accounts can win. The video itself must perform with the test audience.
```

### TikTok Creator Search Insights

URL: https://support.tiktok.com/en/using-tiktok/growing-your-audience/creator-search-insights

Key points used:

- Creators can inspect searched topics, content gaps, related searches, related videos, and post search analytics.

Research implication:

```text
Treat TikTok as both a feed platform and a search platform.
```

## YouTube Shorts

### Shorts analytics: Content tab analytics tips

URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/12942217?co=YOUTUBE._YTVideoType%3Dshorts&hl=en

Key points used:

- YouTube Shorts exposes “Shown in feed.”
- “How many chose to view” measures the percentage of times viewers watched rather than swiped away.

Research implication:

```text
Shorts gives a clean first-frame/hook diagnostic: chose-to-view.
```

### Shorts view-count update

URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/thread/333869549/a-change-to-how-we-count-views-on-shorts?hl=en

Key points used:

- From March 31, 2025, Shorts views count when a Short starts to play or replay.
- YouTube kept the former watch-based metric as “Engaged views” in Analytics.

Research implication:

```text
Raw Shorts views became less useful for creative diagnosis. Prioritise engaged views, chose-to-view, average view duration, average percentage viewed, and subscriber conversion.
```

### YouTube algorithm and search/discovery FAQ

URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/141805?hl=en

Key points used:

- YouTube discovery aims to match viewers with videos they are likely to watch and enjoy.
- Search considers match between title, description, and video content, plus engagement for the query.
- Tags are not important except for misspellings.
- Title/thumbnail changes matter because viewers respond differently, not because the act of changing them magically reranks the video.

Research implication:

```text
Packaging matters because it changes viewer behaviour. Metadata rituals are secondary.
```

## Instagram / Meta

### Instagram Ranking Explained

URL: https://about.instagram.com/blog/announcements/instagram-ranking-explained

Key points used:

- Reels ranking uses user activity such as reels liked, saved, reshared, commented on, and recently engaged with.

Research implication:

```text
Reels strategy should track watch time, saves, sends/reshares, comments, and non-follower performance.
```

### Instagram Trial Reels

URL: https://creators.instagram.com/blog/instagram-trial-reels

Key points used:

- Trial Reels can be shown to non-followers first.
- About 24 hours after sharing, creators can see engagement metrics such as views, likes, comments, and shares.

Research implication:

```text
Trial Reels are useful for testing cold-audience hooks and new formats without relying on follower bias.
```
